reaction_name: 1,3-Dipolar cycloaddition of phenylacetylene with ethyl nitroacetate to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate
bond_formed: C–O and C–C bonds in isoxazole ring
bond_broken: N–O bond rearrangement in nitroacetate during nitrile oxide formation

feed_streams.reasoning: Both substrates are premixed in DES before heating. Premixing ensures the in-situ-generated nitrile oxide is immediately trapped by the dipolarophile, minimizing dimerization to furoxan. Both are stable liquids miscible in DES at room temperature.

reagents.name: Ethyl nitroacetate
reagents.notes: Generates nitrile oxide in situ via thermal dehydration; 2 equiv used to drive conversion

reagents.name: Tetrabutylammonium bromide (TBAB)
reagents.role: Deep eutectic solvent component (hydrogen bond acceptor)
reagents.notes: Forms DES with ethylene glycol; acts as reaction medium promoting the cycloaddition

reagents.name: Ethylene glycol (EG)
reagents.role: Deep eutectic solvent component (hydrogen bond donor)
reagents.notes: Forms DES with TBAB; ~1 mL total DES per 0.5 mmol substrate

mechanism_steps.description: Thermal tautomerization/dehydration of ethyl nitroacetate to generate ethoxycarbonylformonitrile oxide (nitrile oxide intermediate) in situ. The nitro group undergoes aci-nitro tautomerism followed by loss of water.

Concerted [3+2] cycloaddition of the nitrile oxide (1,3-dipole) with phenylacetylene (dipolarophile). The regioselectivity favors 3,5-disubstituted isoxazole (oxygen attacks terminal carbon of alkyne).
Aromatization of the initial 4,5-dihydroisoxazole cycloadduct to the fully aromatic isoxazole product via elimination (or the cycloaddition directly yields the aromatic product from the alkyne).

stream_logic.reasoning: All components are premixed in a single feed stream at room temperature. The nitrile oxide is only generated upon heating in the reactor, so premixing is safe and ensures the dipolarophile is present to trap the transient nitrile oxide immediately upon formation, suppressing dimerization.

mixing_order_reasoning: Single stream: both substrates dissolved in DES at room temperature. No premature reaction occurs below ~80 °C. The nitrile oxide forms only at elevated temperature inside the heated reactor zone, where phenylacetylene is already present to trap it. This maximizes yield and minimizes furoxan byproduct. Note: DES viscosity (~50-100 cP at RT) may require gentle warming of the feed reservoir to ~40-50 °C to facilitate pumping, but this is below the reaction threshold.
deoxygenation_reasoning: No oxygen-sensitive species involved. The reaction proceeds thermally in air.
quench_reasoning: No quench needed inline. The batch protocol quenches with water for workup, but in flow the product stream can be collected directly and worked up offline. No reactive intermediates persist after the heated zone at full conversion.
wavelength_reasoning: This is a purely thermal reaction; no photocatalyst or photochemical activation is involved.
confidence_notes: High confidence in mechanism: well-established nitrile oxide generation from nitroacetate and [3+2] cycloaddition. The DES viscosity is a practical concern for flow — TBAB/EG (1:5) has significant viscosity at room temperature (~50-100 cP), which will increase pressure drop in narrow-bore tubing. A wider-bore coil (e.g., 1.0-1.6 mm ID) or mild preheating of the feed to ~40-50 °C is recommended. Residence time of 15 min at 120 °C is well-suited for flow. Back-pressure regulator (BPR) at ~5-10 bar recommended to prevent solvent boiling (EG bp 197 °C, but water byproduct could cause bubbles). The 2.0 equiv of ethyl nitroacetate accounts for competitive nitrile oxide dimerization; in flow with better mixing and heat transfer, it may be possible to reduce to 1.5 equiv.
